text: This stroller folds in seconds and fits perfectly in our small car trunk. The one-handed fold is a lifesaver when you have a baby in the other arm.
The stroller broke after just 3 months. The folding latch snapped and we could not get it to lock properly. Really disappointed for this price range.
Absolutely love the large storage basket underneath. Fits my diaper bag, groceries, and still has room left. Great value for money.
The wheels handle bumpy pavements and grass surprisingly well. Took it to the park and it glided smoothly the entire time.
Way too heavy to carry up stairs. My husband can manage it but I cannot do it alone. Expected something lighter at this price.
The canopy is huge and provides excellent sun coverage even in the afternoon. My baby stays cool and shaded throughout our walks.
Canopy is too small. On sunny days the baby's legs are fully exposed. We had to buy a separate sun cover which was an extra expense.
Assembly was straightforward. The manual is clear and everything clicked into place in about 20 minutes. No tools required.
The seat padding is very thin. After 30 minutes my toddler was complaining it was uncomfortable. We added an extra cushion.
Customer service was outstanding. A wheel came loose and they sent a replacement part free of charge within 2 days. Very impressed.
The harness is hard to buckle and unbuckle, especially with winter gloves on. It takes too long every single time.
Extremely easy to push with one hand. I was skeptical but the steering is genuinely smooth and responsive.
This is the best stroller we have owned. Lightweight, sturdy, easy to fold. We have used it daily for 18 months with zero issues.
The fabric started peeling after 6 months. Looks terrible and I am worried about the baby touching the exposed material.
Perfect size for tight shop aisles and public transport. Compact enough without sacrificing comfort for the child.
The recline mechanism is smooth and has multiple positions. My newborn can lay flat which is exactly what we needed.
Front wheels wobble at higher walking speeds. It feels unsafe and I have already had one near-tipping incident on a slope.
The cup holder is too small for standard water bottles. My bottle falls over constantly. A minor annoyance but still annoying.
Great price for what you get. Comparable strollers cost twice as much and do not offer significantly better features.
The brake is easy to engage and holds firmly even on slopes. I feel confident leaving the stroller parked on uneven ground.
